task_yaml.task:
I need to choose a framework for our public REST API documentation site.
Requirements (all of these matter):

- syntax-highlighted code blocks in authored guide pages,
- light/dark mode,
- full-text search,
- documentation VERSIONING so that /v1 and /v2 of the API docs can coexist,
- rendering our OpenAPI/Swagger spec as an interactive API reference.

A fixed set of candidate frameworks is provided offline at:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/candidates.json

Each candidate lists, per capability, whether it is `builtin`, available only
via a `plugin` (extra install/config), or `no` (unsupported), with an
evidence note. A capability counts as satisfied if it's builtin OR available
via a plugin; `no` means it can't meet that requirement.

Work out which frameworks satisfy ALL five requirements, then pick exactly
ONE. When two frameworks both qualify, prefer the one where the most
structurally painful requirement to retrofit — VERSIONING — is built in
rather than bolted on. Be careful: the tool that is purpose-built for OpenAPI
is not necessarily a full docs site, and some very trendy options are missing
versioning.

Save:
- `/tmp_workspace/results/framework_choice.json`: your `selected_id`, why it
  meets every requirement (note which are builtin vs plugin), the tie-break
  reasoning, and a brief note on configuring the OpenAPI rendering + the
  versioning.
- `/tmp_workspace/results/framework_evaluation.json`: per-candidate capability
  coverage against the five requirements, keep/reject, and for each rejected
  framework exactly which requirement it fails (with builtin/plugin/no).
- `/tmp_workspace/results/framework_method.json`: which fields/evidence you
  used and how you decided the tie-break (reproducible).

This is fully offline — do not fetch anything from the network.
